$comment: Color tokens. Path = semantic name (e.g. color.text.primary). Code source of truth: packages/ui/src/tokens/tokens.json. Tailwind utilities: see design-system/tokens/README.md.

default.usage: Page background. The base layer that every other surface sits on.
default.notes: Use bg-surface in Tailwind. Pair with text.primary or text.secondary.

raised.notes: In light mode this matches surface.default; the elevation is signalled by shadow.sm, not by hue.

sunken.usage: Inset zones: list striping, sidebars, code blocks, secondary panels.
sunken.notes: Use to visually 'inset' a region without adding a border.

overlay.usage: Modal and sheet scrim. Sits between the surface stack and the dialog.
overlay.notes: Do not recolor per feature. Always paired with bg-surface-raised on the dialog itself.

primary.notes: Contrast vs surface.default — light 16.7:1, dark 15.4:1. Always AAA.

secondary.usage: Labels, sub-headings, de-emphasized but still important text.

muted.usage: Hints, captions, timestamps, helper text in forms.
muted.notes: Contrast vs surface.default — light 4.9:1, dark 4.6:1 (passes AA for body). Avoid at sizes below 14px.

inverse.usage: Text on a deliberately inverted surface (e.g. a dark toast in light mode).
inverse.notes: Rare. If you reach for this, double-check the design first.

onAccent.usage: Text on an accent-filled background — primary buttons, accent badges.
onAccent.notes: Always white in both themes by design. Contrast vs accent.default — light 8.1:1, dark 6.2:1.

default.usage: Primary action color: buttons, links, focus rings.
default.notes: Reserved for action affordances. Do not paint entire surfaces with it.

hover.notes: Light moves darker; dark moves lighter — both increase contrast on hover.

subtle.usage: Accent-tinted backgrounds: selected row, accent badge, info banner.
subtle.notes: Pair with text.primary or accent.default depending on the surface.

success.usage: Set complete, payment success, save confirmation.
success.notes: Same hue in both themes. For text use signal-success; for fills use signal-success/10 + signal-success text.

danger.notes: Contrast vs surface.default — light 5.3:1, dark 5.1:1. Always paired with an icon or label (color is never the only signal).

warning.usage: Soft warnings: 'this will affect future bookings', 'low connection'.
warning.notes: Use sparingly. Not for errors — that's signal.danger.

pr.usage: Reserved for personal-record moments. Reserved means reserved — do not reuse the gold elsewhere.
pr.notes: Fails AA for small body text (3.9:1). Use at >= 18px or 14px-bold, or pair with a 'PR' badge fill (bg-signal-pr/15 text-signal-pr).

subtle.usage: Card hairlines, list dividers, quiet separations.

default.notes: Contrast vs surface.default — light 1.6:1 (form control border allowed at 3:1 against background — verify before use on tinted surfaces).

strong.usage: High-emphasis dividers, focus on dense surfaces, hover state on form controls.

ring.usage: Visible focus indicator on every interactive element.
ring.notes: Available as the default ring color in Tailwind ('ring' utility) and as --color-focus-ring CSS variable. Paired with shadow-focus for the surrounding glow.
